words.definition: A list of items to be discussed at a formal meeting.
words.example: The chair circulated the agenda a week in advance.

words.definition: A written record of the discussions and decisions made at a meeting.
words.example: The secretary read the minutes of the previous meeting.

words.definition: The minimum number of members required to be present for a meeting to conduct valid business.
words.example: No decision could be taken without a quorum.

words.definition: A formal proposal put to a meeting for discussion or vote.
words.example: The motion was carried by a large majority.

words.definition: A firm decision formally adopted by an assembly.
words.example: The board passed a resolution to expand overseas.

words.definition: A minor change or addition designed to improve a text or motion.
words.example: She proposed an amendment to the wording.

words.definition: A rule made by a company or society to control the actions of its members.
words.example: The bylaw required annual audits.

words.definition: An authoritative order or law issued by a governmental body.
words.example: The city council passed a noise ordinance.

words.definition: A rule made and maintained by an authority.
words.example: New safety regulations came into force this year.

words.definition: A written law passed by a legislative body.
words.example: The statute of limitations had expired.

words.definition: A course or principle of action adopted or proposed by an organization.
words.example: The company introduced a flexible-working policy.

words.definition: An established or official way of doing something.
words.example: The refund procedure is set out on our website.

words.definition: The official procedure or system of rules governing affairs.
words.example: Diplomatic protocol requires a formal reply.

words.definition: An official or authoritative instruction.
words.example: The ministry issued a directive to all schools.

words.definition: A written message in business or diplomacy; a memo.
words.example: She sent a memorandum to all department heads.

words.definition: A short written note, especially in an office.
words.example: Please read the memo about parking arrangements.

words.definition: A letter or notice sent to many people.
words.example: A circular announced the change of address.

words.definition: A short official statement or broadcast summary of news.
words.example: The staff bulletin appears every Friday.

words.definition: A displayed sheet giving news or information.
words.example: A notice on the door listed the opening hours.

words.definition: An account given of a matter after investigation or consideration.
words.example: The annual report was published in March.

words.definition: An official report or statement submitted in response to a formal demand.
words.example: Every citizen must file a tax return.

words.definition: A document listing the contents of a shipment or a summary of proceedings.
words.example: The judge worked through a crowded docket.

words.definition: A collection of documents about a particular person, event, or subject.
words.example: The regulator opened a dossier on the firm.

words.definition: A folder or box for keeping loose papers together and in order.
words.example: She retrieved the personnel file from the cabinet.

words.definition: A collection of historical documents or records.
words.example: The company archive contained decades of correspondence.

words.definition: A book or other collection of financial accounts.
words.example: The ledger balanced to the penny.

words.definition: A list or plan showing turns of duty or leave for individuals or groups.
words.example: The duty roster was pinned to the notice board.

words.definition: An official list or record of names or items.
words.example: Please sign the visitors' register at reception.

words.definition: An official record of events during the voyage of a ship or aircraft, or in a business.
words.example: The security guard updated the log every hour.

words.definition: A list of names, especially of members of an organization.
words.example: The clerk called the roll of members.

words.definition: A document listing a ship's or aircraft's cargo, passengers, or crew.
words.example: The customs officer checked the cargo manifest.

words.definition: A complete list of items such as goods in stock.
words.example: The shop takes inventory twice a year.

words.definition: A list of goods sent or services provided with a statement of the sum due.
words.example: The invoice was payable within thirty days.

words.definition: A written or printed acknowledgment of receiving money or goods.
words.example: Keep the receipt for your records.

words.definition: A small printed piece of paper entitling the holder to a discount or exchangeable for goods.
words.example: Attach the voucher to your expense claim.

words.definition: A document issued by a legal or government official authorizing an action.
words.example: The officers arrived with a search warrant.

words.definition: An official order laying claim to the use of property or materials.
words.example: Submit a requisition for new stationery.

words.definition: A formal written request to an authority, signed by many people.
words.example: The petition gathered ten thousand signatures.

words.definition: A formal request to an authority for something.
words.example: She submitted her application before the deadline.

words.definition: A section of extra information added at the end of a document.
words.example: See appendix B for the full data set.

words.definition: Something added or attached, such as a supplementary section of a document.
words.example: The annexe listed all the technical definitions.

words.definition: An item of additional material added at the end of a book or other publication.
words.example: An addendum corrected the earlier figures.

words.definition: An error in printing or writing, especially one noted in a list of corrections.
words.example: An erratum slip was inserted at the front.

words.definition: A preliminary statement in a document explaining its purpose.
words.example: The preamble set out the treaty's aims.

words.definition: A particular and separate article, stipulation, or proviso in a treaty, bill, or contract.
words.example: Clause seven allowed for early termination.

words.definition: A condition or requirement in a legal document.
words.example: The provision limited liability to one year.

words.definition: A condition or requirement that is specified or demanded as part of an agreement.
words.example: There was a stipulation about weekend work.

words.definition: The formal confirmation or approval of something.
words.example: The treaty awaited parliamentary ratification.

words.definition: An act of giving one's public approval or support to someone or something.
words.example: The proposal won the ministry's endorsement.

words.definition: The action of officially agreeing to something or accepting something as satisfactory.
words.example: The plans went to the committee for approval.

words.definition: Permission for something to happen or agreement to do something.
words.example: Written consent is required before any procedure.

words.definition: An act of relinquishing a right or claim, especially in writing.
words.example: She signed a waiver before the skydive.

words.definition: An authoritative warning or order, especially from a court.
words.example: The court issued an injunction against publication.

words.definition: A writ ordering a person to attend a court.
words.example: A subpoena arrived that morning.

words.definition: A written statement confirmed by oath, for use as evidence in court.
words.example: She swore an affidavit before the notary.

words.definition: A signed written agreement to pay a sum of money by a specified date.
words.example: The contractor posted a performance bond.

words.definition: A written or spoken agreement intended to be enforceable by law.
words.example: Both parties signed the contract in triplicate.

words.definition: A formal agreement or promise between two or more parties.
words.example: The lease contained a covenant against subletting.

words.definition: A formally concluded and ratified agreement between countries.
words.example: The peace treaty ended decades of conflict.

words.definition: An official agreement or treaty.
words.example: The two states signed a trade accord.

words.definition: A written grant by a country's legislative or sovereign power, by which an institution such as a company is created.
words.example: The university's royal charter dates from 1832.

words.definition: A body of fundamental principles by which a state or organization is governed.
words.example: Any change must be consistent with the constitution.

words.definition: An official order or commission to do something.
words.example: The agency's mandate covers all public transport.

words.definition: A formal instrument confirming appointment to office.
words.example: A warrant of appointment was presented at the ceremony.

words.definition: An instruction, command, or duty given to a person or group.
words.example: She served on a commission of inquiry.

words.definition: The assignment of authority or responsibility to another person to carry out specific activities.
words.example: Effective delegation frees managers for strategic work.

words.definition: A group of people appointed to undertake a mission or take part in a formal process.
words.example: A deputation met with the minister.

words.definition: The official power to make legal decisions and judgements.
words.example: The case fell outside the tribunal's jurisdiction.

words.definition: The action or fact of complying with a wish or command.
words.example: Regular audits ensure compliance with the standard.

words.definition: The action or manner of governing an organization.
words.example: Good governance requires transparency.

words.definition: A system of government in which most decisions are made by state officials rather than by elected representatives.
words.example: Reforms aimed to reduce needless bureaucracy.

words.definition: A system in which members of an organization or society are ranked according to relative status or authority.
words.example: The organizational hierarchy was clearly defined.

words.definition: A system in an organization by which orders are passed from one level to the next.
words.example: The report followed the chain of command.

words.definition: The particular responsibilities held by a government minister.
words.example: She was given the transport portfolio.

words.definition: The holding of an office; a period for which an office is held.
words.example: His tenure as chairman lasted six years.

words.definition: The holder of an office or post.
words.example: The incumbent stood for re-election.

words.definition: A person who succeeds another in an office or position.
words.example: Her successor took over in September.

words.definition: A person officially proposed for an office or position.
words.example: Each nominee was interviewed in turn.

words.definition: A process of voting, in writing and typically in secret.
words.example: A postal ballot was arranged.

words.definition: The right to vote in public elections.
words.example: Universal adult franchise was won in stages.

words.definition: A general vote by the electorate on a single political question.
words.example: The referendum settled the matter.

words.definition: A meeting of the members of a legislative body who are members of a particular political party.
words.example: The caucus met behind closed doors.

words.definition: A period devoted to a particular activity, especially a meeting of a deliberative body.
words.example: Parliament returned for its autumn session.

words.definition: A period of time occupied by a court, committee, or other body in performing its business.
words.example: The sitting adjourned at six.

words.definition: A period of time when a formal meeting or activity is suspended.
words.example: Congress adjourned for the summer recess.

words.definition: A postponement of a meeting or session.
words.example: The chair moved the adjournment of the meeting.

words.definition: A large formal assembly of people.
words.example: The university held its annual convocation.

words.definition: A group of people gathered together in one place for a common purpose.
words.example: The general assembly ratified the new rules.

words.definition: The group of people who officially direct the business of a company or organization.
words.example: The board approved the acquisition.

words.definition: A group of people appointed for a specific function by a larger group.
words.example: The steering committee met monthly.

words.definition: A committee formed from the main committee.
words.example: A subcommittee was tasked with drafting the report.

words.definition: A small group of experts convened for a specific purpose.
words.example: A panel of judges scored each entry.

words.definition: A group of experts working together to achieve specified goals.
words.example: A working group examined the technical options.

words.definition: A unit or formation established to work on a single defined task or activity.
words.example: A task force was set up to fight fraud.

words.definition: A permanent administrative office or department.
words.example: The secretariat prepared the background papers.

words.definition: The board of directors of a company or the position of director.
words.example: The directorate oversees all research programmes.

words.definition: A business or organization providing a particular service on behalf of another.
words.example: The agency handles all press enquiries.

words.definition: An office or department for transacting particular business.
words.example: The statistics bureau publishes monthly figures.

words.definition: A division of a large organization or building, dealing with a specific area of activity.
words.example: The finance department approved the request.

words.definition: A government department headed by a minister.
words.example: The ministry of education revised the curriculum.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A unit or formation established to work on a single defined task or activity."?
quiz.explanation: Task force (noun) — A unit or formation established to work on a single defined task or activity.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A permanent administrative office or department."?
quiz.explanation: Secretariat (noun) — A permanent administrative office or department.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The board of directors of a company or the position of director."?
quiz.explanation: Directorate (noun) — The board of directors of a company or the position of director.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A business or organization providing a particular service on behalf of another."?
quiz.explanation: Agency (noun) — A business or organization providing a particular service on behalf of another.

quiz.question: Which word means: "An office or department for transacting particular business."?
quiz.explanation: Bureau (noun) — An office or department for transacting particular business.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A division of a large organization or building, dealing with a specific area of activity."?
quiz.explanation: Department (noun) — A division of a large organization or building, dealing with a specific area of activity.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A government department headed by a minister."?
quiz.explanation: Ministry (noun) — A government department headed by a minister.
